seoTitle: Change Beginning Balance in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Fix an incorrect opening balance in QuickBooks Online by editing the Opening Balance Equity entry and updating its reconciliation status.
intro: An incorrect opening balance can throw off every reconciliation that follows. This guide shows how to locate the original Opening Balance Equity entry in QuickBooks Online, correct its value, and mark it as reconciled so your account matches your real bank statement.

steps.heading: Open the account register
steps.body: Go to Transactions, then Chart of Accounts, find your target account, and click View Register.

steps.heading: Find the opening balance entry
steps.body: Scan the register for the very first entry, typically labeled Opening Balance Equity.

steps.heading: Edit the value
steps.body: Click the entry to expand it, then overwrite the deposit or charge amount with your corrected beginning balance.

steps.heading: Confirm the reconciliation status
steps.body: Check the status column and mark the entry as reconciled (R) so QuickBooks treats it as the new starting balance.

steps.heading: Save and verify
steps.body: Click Save, confirm the overwrite warning, and then check your reconciliation dashboard to ensure future closes track correctly.

faq.question: Where is the beginning balance stored?
faq.answer: It's recorded as the first entry in your account register, usually labeled Opening Balance Equity.

faq.question: Will changing the beginning balance affect my past reconciliations?
faq.answer: It can, since it changes the starting point of your ledger, so recheck reconciliations completed after the original setup date.

faq.question: Do I need to mark the entry as reconciled?
faq.answer: Yes, marking it reconciled tells QuickBooks to treat it as your locked-in starting point rather than an open item.

faq.question: What if I don't see a warning pop-up when saving?
faq.answer: QuickBooks typically shows a confirmation prompt for changes to reconciled entries; if it doesn't appear, double-check that your edit actually saved.
